rows.notes: Description '1838.' is clearly visible in the image. Both extractors agreed on header type and no monetary amounts. Overrode gpt-5-mini's '1238'.

The description 'Subscriptions Viz' is a clear reading of the image, where 'Viz' is shorthand for 'videlicet'. Both extractors agreed on header type and no monetary amounts. Overrode gemini-flash's '(Recd)'.

Description and empty amounts are agreed. This row clearly acts as a structural label for the following group of entries, therefore 'header' is the correct row type, aligning with gemini-flash.

Amounts are clear and agreed. The description is a hybrid, combining '1st Qtr' from gemini-flash with 'Xmas' from gpt-5-mini, both of which are accurate visual readings from the image. The trailing dash is also present.
Amounts are clear and agreed. Description '2nd - to Do 1838.' is the correct transcription, using the abbreviation 'Do' as written. This aligns with gemini-flash.

Description '3rd Mich.' is the correct reading. Both extractors agreed on entry type and no individual monetary amount for this line, as it's part of a braced group with the next line. Aligns with gemini-flash.

Description '4th Mich.' is clear. The amount 18 pounds, 10 shillings, 0 pence is visibly associated with the braced group of '3rd Mich.' and '4th Mich.'. Assigning this combined amount to the last entry of the group ('4th Mich.') is the most faithful way to represent the data without inventing descriptive text. This aligns with gemini-flash, and gpt-5-mini's separate 'Total (College Gardener group)' row with an invented description is dropped as an extra row.

Amounts and row type are agreed. The description is accurately represented by the single '£' symbol, which is clearly visible as the label for the final total. gemini-flash correctly extracted just the symbol as the description, whereas gpt-5-mini included the amounts in its description.
